title: Optimize images across the application
body: ### Background Large unoptimized images are slowing down the initial page load time. This can be improved to provide a better user experience. ### Task Implement image optimization techniques across the application. ### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Convert static assets to `.webp` format where applicable. - [ ] Ensure all `` tags have proper `width`, `height`, and `loading="lazy"` attributes. - [ ] Test page load speed using Lighthouse. ### Hints - Check the `public` directory and `src/components/EventCard.tsx`. - Vite handles basic asset optimization, but lazy loading is key.

title: Add debounce to Search Inputs
body:
### Background
Currently, typing in a search bar triggers a filter or network request on every single keystroke. This can be improved to provide better performance.

### Task
Implement a debounce function on the search inputs.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Wrap the search input `onChange` handler in a debounce function.
- [ ] The delay should be `300ms`.
- [ ] Ensure it works flawlessly in the Club Directory.

### Hints
- You can use `useDebounce` from a custom hook or implement it manually using `setTimeout`.
- Check `src/routes/clubs.tsx`.

title: Add a visual character counter to 'Short Description' inputs
body:
### Background
The database has a strict limit on the length of short descriptions, but the UI doesn't show the user how many characters they have left. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Add a character counter below the Short Description input on the Create Event form.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Display text like `45 / 150 characters`.
- [ ] Turn the text red when they are within 10 characters of the limit.
- [ ] Prevent typing past the maximum length using the `maxLength` attribute.

### Hints
- Check `src/routes/create-event.tsx` or wherever the form is located.
- Use React state to track the `length` of the input string.

title: Add global loading spinners for slow network requests
body:
### Background
When navigating between major pages, if data fetching takes time, the app appears frozen. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Implement a global top-bar loading indicator that activates during network requests.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add a slim progress bar at the very top of the window (like YouTube or GitHub).
- [ ] It should start when TanStack Router is in a pending navigation state.
- [ ] It should disappear when the page fully loads.

### Hints
- TanStack Router provides a `useRouterState` hook which exposes `isLoading`.
- You can use a library like `nprogress` or build a custom thin div.

title: Clean up console.logs and debugging statements
body:
### Background
Several `console.log` statements were left in the codebase during development. This can be improved to provide a cleaner production environment.

### Task
Remove all unnecessary `console.log`, `console.warn`, and debugging comments.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search the entire `src` directory for `console.log`.
- [ ] Remove them unless they are explicitly catching and logging critical errors (in which case use `console.error`).
- [ ] Ensure the app builds without errors.

### Hints
- You can use VS Code's global search (`Ctrl+Shift+F`) to find them all.

title: Implement Real-time Comments using Supabase Subscriptions
body:
### Background
Users have to refresh the page to see new comments on a discussion post. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Use Supabase Realtime to automatically push new comments to the UI.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Subscribe to `INSERT` events on the `comments` table.
- [ ] When a new comment arrives, append it to the local React state or update the React Query cache.
- [ ] Ensure the subscription is properly cleaned up when the component unmounts.

### Hints
- Read the Supabase documentation on `supabase.channel('public:comments').on(...)`.
- Check `src/routes/discussions.$postId.tsx`.

title: Show 'Live Active Users' count on the dashboard
body:
### Background
It would be cool to see how many people are currently browsing the app. This can be improved to provide a better community feel.

### Task
Implement a simple live presence indicator using Supabase Presence.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Connect to a global Supabase Presence channel.
- [ ] Track users joining and leaving the app.
- [ ] Display a small badge on the navbar showing "X users online".

### Hints
- Read the Supabase Presence documentation.
- You can use an anonymous connection for users who are not logged in.

title: Add Zod schema validation to User Profile updates
body:
### Background
The 'Edit Profile' form does not have robust validation for fields like LinkedIn URLs or Phone Numbers. This can be improved to provide better data integrity.

### Task
Implement Zod validation for the Edit Profile form.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create a Zod schema in `src/lib/schemas.ts`.
- [ ] Validate that URLs are actually valid URLs.
- [ ] Validate that names are at least 2 characters long.
- [ ] Integrate this schema with `react-hook-form` using `@hookform/resolvers/zod`.

### Hints
- Check how validation is handled in the `Login` or `Signup` forms as an example.

title: Refine error messages across authentication forms
body:
### Background
When login or signup fails, the error messages are often technical and hard for users to understand (e.g., "Auth session missing"). This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Map common Supabase Auth errors to user-friendly error messages.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create a utility function `getFriendlyAuthError(error: Error)`.
- [ ] Handle 'Invalid login credentials', 'User already registered', etc.
- [ ] Display these friendly messages in the form errors instead of the raw `error.message`.

### Hints
- Check `src/routes/login.tsx` and `src/routes/signup.tsx`.

title: Add a 'Show/Hide Password' toggle button
body:
### Background
Users often mistype their passwords but cannot verify what they typed because it is masked. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Add an eye icon to password inputs that toggles visibility.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add an icon button inside the right side of the password input.
- [ ] Clicking it toggles the input type between `password` and `text`.
- [ ] Use `lucide-react` for the Eye and EyeOff icons.

### Hints
- You may need to create a custom `PasswordInput` component wrapping the standard `Input`.

title: Implement a Calendar View for Events
body:
### Background
Events are only visible in a standard list format. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Add a 'Calendar View' toggle to the Events page that displays events on a monthly calendar.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Integrate a library like `react-big-calendar` or `fullcalendar-react`.
- [ ] Add a toggle button group to switch between "List" and "Calendar" views.
- [ ] Populate the calendar with the fetched events.

### Hints
- This is a larger feature! Make sure to lazy load the calendar library if it is heavy.

title: Generate QR Codes for Event RSVPs
body:
### Background
Organizers have no easy way to check people in at the door. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Generate a unique QR code for users when they RSVP to an event.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] When an RSVP is successful, generate a QR code containing the `rsvp_id`.
- [ ] Display the QR code on a "Ticket" modal.
- [ ] Use a lightweight library like `qrcode.react`.

### Hints
- You can add a "View Ticket" button to the Event Card for events the user is attending.

title: Add Map Integration for Event Locations
body:
### Background
Locations are currently just text strings, making it hard to find exactly where an event is. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Embed a map on the Event Details page if a valid location is provided.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Use Google Maps Embed API or an open-source alternative like Leaflet/OpenStreetMap.
- [ ] Render an iframe passing the event location string as the query parameter.
- [ ] Hide the map if the event is "Online".

### Hints
- `https://www.google.com/maps?q={location}&output=embed` is a quick way to embed an iframe.

title: Add Social Sharing buttons (Twitter, LinkedIn, WhatsApp)
body:
### Background
We only have a 'Copy Link' button, but no direct integration with social platforms. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Add dedicated social sharing buttons to Event and Discussion pages.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add buttons for Twitter, LinkedIn, and WhatsApp.
- [ ] Use standard URL sharing formats (e.g., `https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=...`).
- [ ] Use appropriate brand colors on hover.

### Hints
- Check out `react-share` or just build the anchor tags manually.

title: Implement an 'Add to Google Calendar' button
body:
### Background
Users often forget about events they RSVP'd to. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Add a button that generates an `.ics` file or a Google Calendar direct link.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Format a Google Calendar URL with the event title, start time, end time, and description.
- [ ] Open the link in a new tab (`target="_blank"`).
- [ ] Display this button prominently after a user RSVPs.

### Hints
- The URL format looks like: `https://calendar.google.com/calendar/render?action=TEMPLATE&text=...`

title: Create a 'My RSVPs' Dashboard tab
body:
### Background
Users have to manually search for events to remember what they signed up for. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Add a dedicated section in the user dashboard to view all upcoming and past RSVPs.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create a new route `/dashboard/rsvps`.
- [ ] Fetch and display events joined with the user's `rsvps`.
- [ ] Add tabs to filter between "Upcoming" and "Past" events.

### Hints
- You will need a Supabase query that joins `events` and `rsvps` where `user_id` matches.

title: Implement Admin Role to Delete Any Post
body:
### Background
Currently, only the author of a post can delete it. We need moderation capabilities. This can be improved to provide better community guidelines.

### Task
Allow users with the `ADMIN` role to delete any discussion post or comment.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Update the frontend to show the 'Delete' button if `user.role === 'ADMIN'`.
- [ ] Crucially: Update the Supabase RLS policies to allow DELETE if the user's role is admin.

### Hints
- You will need to write a custom PostgreSQL function in Supabase to check the user's role for the RLS policy.
labels: enhancement, frontend, backend, security, good-backend, ECSoC26

title: Add 'Pull to Refresh' on mobile devices
body:
### Background
Mobile users expect to pull down on feeds to refresh data, but this is not implemented natively. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Implement a pull-to-refresh interaction on the main event and discussion feeds.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Use a library or custom touch event handlers to detect a downward drag at the top of the page.
- [ ] Trigger React Query's `invalidateQueries` when the threshold is met.
- [ ] Show a loading spinner during the refresh.

### Hints
- Check out libraries like `react-simple-pull-to-refresh`.

title: Refactor Event Card into smaller sub-components
body:
### Background
The `EventCard.tsx` file is getting very large and hard to maintain. This can be improved to provide a better developer experience.

### Task
Break down the `EventCard` component into smaller, reusable pieces.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Extract the Date badge into a `EventDateBadge` component.
- [ ] Extract the RSVP button logic into an `EventRSVPButton` component.
- [ ] Ensure no functionality is broken.

### Hints
- Keep the extracted components in the same folder if they are only used by `EventCard`, or move them to `ui/` if globally useful.

title: Add missing Markdown rendering support for Club Descriptions
body:
### Background
We added Markdown for discussions, but Club descriptions are still plain text. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Render Club Descriptions using `react-markdown`.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Import `react-markdown` in the Club Details page.
- [ ] Render the description safely, applying Prose typography styles.
- [ ] Update the Club Creation form to use a simple Markdown editor or textarea with preview.

### Hints
- You can reuse the Markdown components implemented for the discussions feature.

title: Add Unit Tests for standard UI components
body:
### Background
Our base UI components (Buttons, Inputs, Badges) don't have tests to catch accidental styling changes. This can be improved to provide a better developer experience.

### Task
Write basic rendering tests for the Radix UI wrapper components.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Install `@testing-library/react` if not present.
- [ ] Write tests for `Button`, `Badge`, and `Input`.
- [ ] Verify that they render correctly with different variants (e.g., `primary`, `destructive`).

### Hints
- Look into Vitest + React Testing Library integration.

title: Create a 'Not Found' 404 Page
body:
### Background
When users navigate to a route that doesn't exist, they see a generic or blank page. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Design and implement a beautiful 404 page.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Use TanStack Router's `NotFoundRoute` feature.
- [ ] Design a fun, CampusConnect-themed 404 page (maybe a lost mascot or a funny message).
- [ ] Include a prominent "Return to Home" button.

### Hints
- Read the TanStack Router documentation on handling 404s.

title: Implement an Error Boundary component
body:
### Background
If a React component throws an error, the entire application crashes to a white screen. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Wrap the application in a React Error Boundary to gracefully catch and display errors.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create an `ErrorBoundary` class component or use a library like `react-error-boundary`.
- [ ] Display a friendly "Something went wrong" message with a button to reload the page.
- [ ] Wrap the root route or critical sections of the app.

### Hints
- Check React documentation for Error Boundaries.

title: Add a visual progress bar during file uploads
body:
### Background
When users upload large images (like profile pictures or event banners), there is no indication of progress, making the app feel frozen. This can be improved to provide a better user experience.

### Task
Display a progress bar indicating the upload percentage.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Utilize the `onUploadProgress` event from the Supabase storage client (or underlying fetch/axios).
- [ ] Display a Radix UI `Progress` component that fills up as the file uploads.
- [ ] Hide it immediately when the upload completes.

### Hints
- Supabase's `storage.from().upload()` does not natively support progress, you may need to use TUS resumable uploads or a custom XMLHttpRequest for progress tracking.

title: Enforce strict TypeScript checks in CI
body:
### Background
Our GitHub Actions CI runs ESLint and Prettier, but doesn't explicitly run a full TypeScript compiler check. This can be improved to provide better code quality.

### Task
Add `tsc --noEmit` to the CI pipeline to catch type errors before merging PRs.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Update the `build-and-lint` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`.
- [ ] Add a step that runs `bun run typecheck` (which maps to `tsc --noEmit`).
- [ ] Fix any existing type errors that this uncovers!

### Hints
- This will make the repository significantly more stable.
